task_completion.description: The agent opens or accesses the original image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The original image (a photograph) is visible on screen, either opened in an image viewer, an image editor (like GIMP), or referenced in a script.

task_completion.description: The agent initiates an offset operation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An offset tool/dialog is open in an image editor (e.g., Layer -> Transform -> Offset in GIMP), or a script is being written with functions to shift/roll the image.

task_completion.description: The agent inputs the correct offset dimensions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The values 150 (for X, width, or right) and 100 (for Y, height, or down) are typed into the offset dialog input fields or explicitly defined in the script.

task_completion.description: The agent enables the wrap-around behavior.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Wrap around', 'Wrap', or similar edge-behavior checkbox/radio button is selected in the GUI, or wrap-around logic (such as numpy.roll) is visible in the code.

task_completion.description: The final offset image is generated and displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The resulting image is visible on screen, showing the photographic content shifted to the right and downwards, with the parts that were pushed off the edge seamlessly appearing on the left and top edges.

integrity.description: The agent actually performed the offset operation rather than faking the result.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a progression of opening the file, applying the transformation via software or script, and saving/viewing the result, rather than just renaming an existing file or downloading a pre-made solution.

integrity.description: The resulting image contains the same content as the original.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final image is clearly the same Unsplash photograph as the original, just shifted. It should not be a blank canvas, a solid color, or a completely different picture.

integrity.description: The image dimensions were preserved during the offset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final image does not appear cropped, and the canvas size has not expanded. There should be no empty, white, or transparent borders around the image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires offsetting an image by exactly 150 pixels to the right and 100 pixels down. Wrap-around must be enabled, meaning pixels pushed off the right and bottom edges must reappear on the left and top edges respectively. The original image is an 800x600 (or 800x608) Unsplash photo. The final image dimensions must remain identical to the original.
